name: ExtensionObservationDefinition_BodySite
title: R5: Body part to be observed (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `ObservationDefinition.bodySite` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`ObservationDefinition.bodySite` 0..1 `CodeableConcept`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `ObservationDefinition.bodySite` 0..1 `CodeableConcept`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `ObservationDefinition.bodySite` has a context of ObservationDefinition based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `ObservationDefinition`.
Element `ObservationDefinition.bodySite` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: Body part to be observed (new)
element.comment:
Element `ObservationDefinition.bodySite` has a context of ObservationDefinition based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `ObservationDefinition`.
Element `ObservationDefinition.bodySite` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
Only used if the defined observation is to be made directly on a body part and if this body part is not implied by code found in ObservationDefinition.code. Not used for in vitro diagnostic observations. 
Example: 14975008 |Forearm structure|.

element.definition: The site on the subject's body where the  observation is to be made.
element.comment:
Only used if the defined observation is to be made directly on a body part and if this body part is not implied by code found in ObservationDefinition.code. Not used for in vitro diagnostic observations. 
Example: 14975008 |Forearm structure|.
